title: Sprites, Groups, Update Contracts, Kill Semantics, and Entity Ownership · Behavior Case: Layered Groups
summary: Sprites add themselves to global groups, update signatures differ, draw order depends on set iteration, killed objects keep external references, and restart doubles group membership. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the layered groups increment in reconciled sprite and group lifecycle. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.
Understand kill as group removal, release external indexes and resources, and reconcile created, active, and retired counts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-sprites-groups-ownership-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-sprites-groups-ownership-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_sprites_groups_ownership_5(created: int = 20, active: int = 13, killed: int = 7, group_memberships_released: bool = True, update_before_draw: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(created, active, killed) < 0:
        return False, active, "group-domain"
    if active + killed != created:
        return False, active, "lifecycle-reconciliation"
    if not group_memberships_released:
        return False, killed, "group-leak"
    if not update_before_draw:
        return False, active, "stale-render"
    assert active + killed == created
    return True, active, "owned-sprites"

    if not group_memberships_released:

    assert active + killed == created
    return True, active, "owned-sprites"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Layered Groups: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6769 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Layered Groups model · Theory Layered Groups.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Layered Groups model · Theory Layered Groups, keep Layered Groups connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Layered Groups model · Theory Layered Groups, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Layered Groups precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Layered Groups model · Theory Layered Groups, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 6769 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Layered Groups model · Theory Layered Groups, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Current group iteration order is a stable rendering contract.
